compatibleScales.musicalContext: B Locrian is the vii° mode in C major. Contains all diminished chord tones. Creates leading tone function to I.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use sparingly for leading tone tension. Creates strong pull to I. Common in classical and jazz for dramatic resolutions.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Highly unstable - resolves strongly to C. The B is leading tone to C. Use for dramatic tension before I resolution.

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Whole-half diminished provides tensions for diminished chords. Symmetrical construction. Advanced jazz concept.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use for advanced jazz vii° approaches. Very dissonant. Perfect for bebop and modern jazz. Maximum tension.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Very dissonant and tense. Use for maximum tension before resolution. Creates symmetrical, angular melodies.

compatibleScales.relationship: Source of vii° in minor keys
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bdim comes from C harmonic minor. Creates classical minor key resolution. The B is raised 7th of C minor.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use for classical or neoclassical approaches. Perfect for minor key contexts. Traditional leading tone resolution.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Classical approach to diminished. The B leads to C in minor contexts. Traditional classical resolution.
